resources:
OpenCode Android Unofficial open-source client
Home Chat Connections Sessions Workspaces Activity Settings
Connect a PC to get started Connected · %s On the PC, start opencode serve with a password, then enter its LAN or Tailscale address. Add connection Edit connection Display name OpenCode server URL Username Server password Allow cleartext HTTP on LAN/Tailscale Test connection Select Active Not set Delete Cancel Save Refresh
New chat Message OpenCode… Send message Stop Thinking… Processing… Add and select a PC connection before starting a chat. Model Agent Thinking Context %1$d%% Context unavailable Additional settings Attachment: %1$s Approval required Allow once Always allow Reject
Recent sessions View all No sessions yet
PC remote runtime Android local runtime Managed local runtime Install, start, stop, and repair the Android-local OpenCode runtime from Workspaces.
Home assistant Set as default digital assistant Voice Microphone permission is required for voice features Read responses aloud Continuous conversation Start listening again after each response Could not open assistant settings
About
Approvals Task status OpenCode needs approval OpenCode finished Session %1$s is idle OpenCode error A session failed Provider connections Connect providers through the selected OpenCode runtime. Available sign-in methods are supplied by OpenCode. Provider id (e.g. openai) API key Save key Clear key OAuth authentication Authorization code Complete authentication in the browser, then tap Complete. Complete Saved Not set Connect %1$s Choose a sign-in method. Connect Change connection Disconnect Connected Not connected Provider connected. Provider disconnected. No provider authentication methods are available from the selected OpenCode runtime. Waiting for authorization to finish in the browser… Confirmation code Connecting… Provider authentication failed. Try again. Continue Assistant runtime Assistant workspace path Choose the OpenCode runtime, workspace, model, and agent used when Android launches this app as the default assistant. Assistant model Assistant agent Loading models from the assistant runtime… Use chat defaults Speak the final OpenCode answer with Android text-to-speech. Import folder into local workspace Notifications are used for approvals and task completion while the app is in the background. OpenCode %1$s Allow notifications Attach file Tool Command Reasoning Copy Share Retry Jump to latest %1$d files changed Search models… Recent No models match your search Search sessions… Rename Delete session? "%1$s" and its history will be permanently deleted. No sessions match your search Switch session Approvals Auto-allow read-only tools Skip the approval prompt for read/list/search/grep/glob tool calls. Edits, deletes, and shell commands always ask. Appearance Theme System Light Dark Use wallpaper colors (Material You) On Android 12+, derive the app\'s colors from your wallpaper instead of the OpenCode palette. Pending approval Running now Skip Next Control OpenCode from your phone Run OpenCode entirely on this Android device Or connect to OpenCode running on your Mac, Linux, or Windows PC Talk to it with push-to-talk voice and hear responses read aloud How do you want to start? Use this device Set up a self-contained OpenCode runtime on your phone. No PC required. Connect a PC Control OpenCode already running on your computer over LAN or Tailscale. Show the intro again Paste connection QR / link Discover on LAN No OpenCode services found yet Hand off session Optional wake-word pack Not bundled. Install a maintainer-signed HTTPS phrase pack. The signature, size, SHA-256, and ZIP contents are verified before activation. Pack installed: %1$s %2$s No wake-word pack installed Remove pack\n Install signed pack\n Signed manifest URL\n Enter the HTTPS URL of a JSON manifest signed for OpenCode Android.\n Wake-word listening\n Keeps a microphone foreground service and persistent notification active. It does not start automatically after reboot.\n Verifying and installing pack…
